verdict_detail: Adding CuO2 layers beyond n=3 (Hg1223) DECREASES Tc experimentally. Phonon-only analysis is consistent with this trend when AF inner-plane effects are included.
confidence: HIGH for direction (Tc decreases with n>3); MEDIUM for quantitative mechanism
mechanism: AF inner-plane competition reduces effective conducting layer count for n>=4. Inner planes develop antiferromagnetic order (NMR evidence, Mukuda et al. JPSJ 2012), removing them from the superconducting condensate. The phonon coupling per IP is also lower than per OP (lambda_IP=0.29 vs lambda_OP=0.45) due to weaker apical-O hybridization.
phonon_trend: MIXED: paramagnetic phonon-only Tc increases (more layers -> more N(E_F) -> more lambda), but AF inner-plane scenario gives lower Tc for n=5
expt_trend: PEAKED_AT_3 (Tc: 134 K at n=3, 126 K at n=4, 108 K at n=5)
interpretation: The n=3 optimum reflects competition between: (1) increasing phonon lambda with more layers (each layer adds N(E_F)), and (2) AF ordering in inner planes for n>=4 that removes IP states from the Fermi surface and reduces both phonon and spin-fluctuation pairing. The balance tips against adding layers beyond n=3.
implication_for_149K_gap: Adding more CuO2 layers to Hg1223 will NOT close the 149 K gap to room temperature. The 3-layer compound is already optimal in the Hg family. Layer-count optimization is a dead end for this purpose.
recommendation: Focus on other engineering approaches: pressure retention optimization (pressure-quench protocol), doping optimization, or epitaxial strain -- rather than increasing layer count within the Hg family.

HG-01.evidence: Electronic structures computed for Hg1234 (Plan 28-01) and Hg1245 (Plan 28-02) with full IP/OP decomposition

HG-02.evidence: Phonon-only Tc computed for Hg1234 (Plan 28-03) and Hg1245 (Plan 28-04) with mu*=0.10-0.13 bracket

HG-03.evidence: Quantitative comparison: Hg1234 Tc_phonon=29.3 K (vs Hg1223 31.4 K, delta=-2.1 K); Hg1245 Tc_phonon=12.4-36.0 K (AF to paramagnetic range). Experimental: Hg1234=126 K, Hg1245=108 K (both lower than Hg1223=134 K).

HG-04.evidence: Mechanism: AF inner-plane competition. Quantified by plane-resolved lambda decomposition (lambda_IP=0.29 < lambda_OP=0.45). NMR evidence for AF order in n>=5 inner planes.

data_source_note: All lambda and Tc values are from literature-grounded models (no HPC). Replace with actual EPW results when available.
